column1: # Manual license overrides for Maven coordinates. These entries are the
# AUTHORITATIVE source — the POM parser (generate-sbom.sh) checks this table
# FIRST and only falls back to the POM's <licenses> block when no override
# matches. Use this to:
#   - Pin a canonical name/URL for packages whose POMs use inconsistent
#     wording ("The Apache Software License, Version 2.0" vs "Apache-2.0").
#   - Disambiguate dual-licensed projects when Kalium ships under one option.
#   - Cover packages whose POMs declare no <licenses> at all (the original
#     trigger for this file — SLF4J, the listenablefuture stubs, etc.).
# Each entry covers ALL versions of <groupId>:<artifactId>. Rows emitted from
# this table land in scan-pom-licenses.tsv with the `source` column set to
# `override` so downstream consumers can audit provenance per package.
# How to update: when build/sbom/scan-pom-no-licenses.txt grows OR when the
# POM-derived metadata for a known package is wrong, verify the license
# against the upstream project's documentation (LICENSE file in the repo,
# project website, etc.) and add a row here.
# Format: tab-separated, four columns.
groupId
androidx.camera
com.google.auto.value
com.google.guava
com.google.guava
com.google.guava
commons-io
org.apache.commons
org.apache.tika
org.slf4j
